chief_complaint: An elderly gentleman presented with acute heart failure symptoms.
history: The patient was previously well before presenting with symptoms of heart failure. There is no detailed past medical history, medications, or social history provided.
exam: No specific vital signs or physical examination findings are provided.
labs: Echocardiography (ECHO) findings were suspicious for cardiac amyloidosis. Cardiac magnetic resonance imaging (CMR) and laboratory results supported the diagnosis. Technetium-99m pyrophosphate (99mTc-PYP) bone scintigraphy with single photon emission computed tomography/computed tomography (SPECT/CT) showed radiotracer uptake in the myocardium higher than the skeletal bone uptake, corresponding to a Perugini score of 3 and an increased heart to contralateral lung ratio (H:CL) of 1.69.
